results.title: Sony Announces $3 Billion Buyback as Memory Prices Take Toll - Bloomberg.com
results.content: said it will buy back as much as ¥500 billion ($3.2 billion) of its shares after rising memory prices weighed on the entertainment group’s annual outlook. For the year through March 2027, the Tokyo-based electronics maker expects an operating profit of ¥1.6 trillion, roughly an 11% increase and in line with analyst estimates. Sony is focusing on improving profitability to combat rising costs of materials, including a plan to set up a new joint venture with Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. results.title: Sony (SONY): Japan Hardware & IP Tech Giant - Securities.io
results.content: Sony is a diversified technology and intellectual-property company whose earnings are driven less by traditional consumer electronics and more by gaming platforms, image sensors, and high-margin music and content rights. Sony is a company that has revolutionized entertainment, gaming, and hardware several times with products like the Walkman or the PlayStation. It is today a leader in sensors, was active in the PC market until 2014, in lithium batteries until 2017, and went into movie making and music (Sony Pictures and Sony Music), including with hits like Spider-Man, a segment where it is still active (see below). **How to read this:** “Revenue Weight” and “Profit Contribution” are directional to highlight why Sony can look like a hardware company on revenue mix, while its earnings are increasingly influenced by platform services and IP-heavy segments. In the medium and long-term, the arrival of the PlayStation 6 in 2027, new IPs, including in the ever more popular anime genre, and massive use of CMOS sensors by drones and AI systems (including self-driving cars) will be the largest growth driver for Sony Group.

results.title: Sony Music Publishing Acquires Recognition Music Group’s Complete Catalog - Billboard
results.content: Following reports that Sony Music Publishing was looking to acquire Recognition Music Group's catalog from Blackstone, the companies formally announced the deal. Sony Music Publishing, in a partnership with Singapore sovereign investment fund GIC, confirmed its agreement to acquire the music assets of the Recognition Music Group from Blackstone, the private equity firm. Beginning in 2021, Blackstone amassed some $3 billion in music assets through forming a partnership with Hipgnosis Songs Capital, which acquired Hipgnosis Songs Management and also invested about $800 million in acquiring music rights; and then in 2024 acquiring the publicly traded Hipgnosis Songs Capital fund for $1.6 billion and assuming some $600 million in debt for a total price of about $2.2 billion. “Our investment in this extraordinary catalog reflects our belief in the enduring power of great music — a belief that resonates deeply throughout Sony Music Group and is shared by our partners at GIC,” Sony Music Publishing chairman & CEO **Jon Platt** said in a statement.

results.title: Kevin Spacey’s Contested Exit From ‘House Of Cards’ Blows Up On MRC In $100M Insurance Trial - Deadline

Yet, with a jury swiftly delivering a strong win for Fireman’s Fund this week over MRC after a more than month long trial , it was indeed once again all about Kevin Spacey. Just last week, Spacey’s manager Evan Lowenstein praised his client online over Sony distributed Spanish project, with a retweet and comment from the actor. Lowenstein did not reply to Deadline’s request for comment on the verdict, so we may have to wait until Spacey himself takes to social media or cable TV. Reps for MRC and their Kinsella Holley Iser Kump Steinsapir lawyers did not respond either. The company will be hauling someone, be it it Spacey or Fireman’s Fund will be back in court sooner or later – or that you can build a very stable house of cards.
